MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-18-2017 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY ALL PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. STEPHENS INSTITUTE IS THE PROPER PARTY TO SEEK ARBITRATION AND THE DECLARATION OF TORIE TASKER SUFFICIENTLY ESTABLISHES THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. THE COURT NEED NOT ADDRESS PLAINTIFF'S OTHER ISSUES SUCH AS FRAUD AND UNCONSCIONABILITY BECAUSE THOSE ARE MATTERS BEFORE THE ARBITRATOR. STEPHENS INSTITUTE IS NOT ENTITLED TO A STATEMENT OF DECISION BECAUSE THE COURT IS COMPELLING ARBITRATION. [THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING IS REFLECTED IN THE COURT REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT.] PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: SUZANNE R. BOLANOS; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: GORDON F. AIAVAO, CSR # 11216, EMAIL: GAIAVAO@SFTC.ORG.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS COLE WILSON AND LANAY LARSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. EVEN THOUGH WILSON AND LARSON ARE NOT SIGNATORIES TO ARBITRATION, THEY ARE ENTITLED TO COMPEL ARBITRATION BASED UPON THE PRINCIPLES OF ESTOPPEL AND AGENCY. THE COURT ORDERS ALL CLAIMS AGAINST THE INDIVIDUALS TO BE ARBITRATED. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-23-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-22-2017 Continued To Apr-11-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings
Off Calendar Aug-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jennifer J. Walt, Esq. of Littler Mendelson PC, Tel. 415.677.3137, for moving party DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE;
Mark S. Perelman, Esq. of Murphy Peason Bradley & Feeney, Tel. 415.788.1900, for defendants Larson & Wilson;
Anthony M. Perez, Esq. of Perez Law Offices, Tel. 916.441.0500, for plaintiff and opposing party.

Prior to the hearing, the Court read into the record the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE's Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings.

RULING: The matter was argued. After arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE's Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings is granted. Stephens Institute is the proper party to seek arbitration and the declaration of Torie Tasker sufficiently establishes the arbitration agreement. The court need not address plaintiff's other issues such as fraud and unconscionability because those are matters before the arbitrator. Stephens Institute is not entitled to a statement of decision because the court is compelling arbitration. [The Court's complete ruling is reflected in the court reporter's transcript.] Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR # 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Aug-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Mark S. Perelman, Esq. of Murphy Peason Bradley & Feeney, Tel. 415.788.1900, for moving party DEFENDANTS COLE WILSON and LANAY LARSON;
Anthony M. Perez, Esq. of Perez Law Offices, Tel. 916.441.0500, for plaintiff and opposing party;
Jennifer J. Walt, Esq. of Littler Mendelson PC, Tel. 415.677.3137, for defendant Stephens Institute.

Prior to the hearing, the Court read into the record the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS COLE WILSON and LANAY LARSON's Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings.

RULING: The matter was argued. After arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS COLE WILSON and LANAY LARSON's Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings is granted. Even though Wilson and Larson are not signatories to arbitration, they are entitled to compel arbitration based upon the principles of estoppel and agency. The court orders all claims against the individuals to be arbitrated. [The Court's complete ruling is reflected in the court reporter's transcript.] Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR # 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings
